context: Tests whether the agent generates a TikTok CSV catalog feed with the correct required fields, keeps descriptions under 500 characters, uses the facebook-nodejs-business-sdk for CAPI, enables server-side tracking to complement the Pixel, sends event_id for deduplication, and correctly populates contentIds from SKUs.

checklist.name: TikTok feed is CSV
checklist.description: generateTikTokCatalogFeed sets Content-Type to 'text/csv' and returns comma-separated rows (not XML or JSON)

checklist.name: TikTok CSV header fields
checklist.description: The CSV header row contains exactly these fields (in any order): sku_id, title, description, availability, condition, price, link, image_link, brand

checklist.name: TikTok sku_id from SKU
checklist.description: The 'sku_id' column in the TikTok feed is populated from the variant's SKU value (not an internal numeric ID)

checklist.name: TikTok description under 500 chars
checklist.description: Descriptions in the TikTok feed are truncated to 500 characters or fewer (e.g. .slice(0, 500) or .substring(0, 500))

checklist.name: facebook-nodejs-business-sdk used
checklist.description: meta-capi.ts imports from 'facebook-nodejs-business-sdk' (specifically ServerEvent, EventRequest, UserData, and/or CustomData classes) — not a raw fetch() call to the CAPI endpoint

checklist.name: CAPI + Pixel rationale documented
checklist.description: INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md explains that server-side CAPI is used alongside the browser Pixel to recover events lost to ad blockers (mentions the ~20-40% gap or similar reasoning)

checklist.name: event_id deduplication
checklist.description: sendMetaPurchaseEvent includes an event_id in the ServerEvent (or INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md explains that the same event_id must be sent in both Pixel and CAPI calls for deduplication)

checklist.name: contentType set to product
checklist.description: CustomData in sendMetaPurchaseEvent sets content_type (or calls .setContentType()) to 'product'

checklist.name: contentIds from SKUs
checklist.description: CustomData sets content_ids (or calls .setContentIds()) using the SKU values from order.lineItems (not product IDs or order IDs)

checklist.name: TikTok shop prerequisites noted
checklist.description: INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md mentions that TikTok requires a separate seller account approval and/or shop connection — catalog sync alone is not sufficient to appear in TikTok Shop
